patch:
diff --git a/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineAndOrXor.cpp b/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineAndOrXor.cpp
index 6e4689863407..9b5b4979df15 100644
--- a/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineAndOrXor.cpp
+++ b/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineAndOrXor.cpp
@@ -1325,6 +1325,7 @@ Value *InstCombinerImpl::foldAndOrOfICmpsUsingRanges(ICmpInst *ICmp1,
   const APInt *C1, *C2;
   if (!match(ICmp1, m_ICmp(Pred1, m_Value(V1), m_APInt(C1))) ||
       !match(ICmp2, m_ICmp(Pred2, m_Value(V2), m_APInt(C2))))
+
     return nullptr;
 
   // Look through add of a constant offset on V1, V2, or both operands. This
@@ -1355,12 +1356,100 @@ Value *InstCombinerImpl::foldAndOrOfICmpsUsingRanges(ICmpInst *ICmp1,
   Value *NewV = V1;
   std::optional<ConstantRange> CR = CR1.exactUnionWith(CR2);
   if (!CR) {
+    // Handle the specific case: (X & -32) != 128 && X != 127  -->  (X - 160) u< -33
+    if (IsAnd && Pred1 == ICmpInst::ICMP_NE && Pred2 == ICmpInst::ICMP_NE &&
+        ICmp1 && ICmp2 && C1 && C2) {
+      const APInt *AndMask = nullptr;
+      Value *X = nullptr;
+      if (match(ICmp1->getOperand(0), m_And(m_Value(X), m_APInt(AndMask))) &&
+          X == ICmp2->getOperand(0)) {
+        // Check if the AND mask is a negative power of two (like -32)
+        if (AndMask->isStrictlyNegative() && (-*AndMask).isPowerOf2()) {
+          APInt Mask = -*AndMask; // positive power of two, e.g., 32
+          APInt AndValue = *C1;   // value being compared to (X & -M), e.g., 128
+          APInt XValue = *C2;     // value being compared to X, e.g., 127
+
+          // We want: (X & ~Mask+1) == AndValue  -->  X in [AndValue, AndValue + Mask - 1]
+          // But condition is NE, so we are saying X not in [AndValue, AndValue + Mask - 1]
+          // And separately X != XValue.
+          // If XValue == AndValue - 1, then the combined range becomes X not in [AndValue - 1, AndValue + Mask - 1]
+          // i.e., X - AndValue u< -(Mask + 1) covers the gap?
+
+          // Special case: when XValue + 1 == AndValue, then we have:
+          // X != AndValue - 1 and (X & -Mask) != AndValue  => X not in [AndValue, AndValue + Mask - 1]
+          // So overall X not in [AndValue - 1, AndValue + Mask - 1]
+          if (XValue + 1 == AndValue) {
+            APInt LowerBound = XValue; // = AndValue - 1
+            APInt UpperBound = AndValue + Mask - 1;
+            APInt RangeSize = UpperBound - LowerBound + 1;
+
+            // We can express: !((X - LowerBound) < RangeSize) as (X - LowerBound) u>= RangeSize
+            // But we want to return true when X is NOT in the interval, so we want:
+            // (X - LowerBound) u>= RangeSize  -->  icmp ult (X - LowerBound), RangeSize is false
+            // So we want: icmp uge (X - LowerBound), RangeSize
+            // But we can use: icmp ult (X - LowerBound), ~RangeSize + 1? No.
+
+            // Actually, we want to return true if X < LowerBound or X > UpperBound.
+            // That is: (X - LowerBound) u> (UpperBound - LowerBound)
+            // i.e., (X - LowerBound) u< -(UpperBound - LowerBound + 1) is false.
+
+            APInt Range = UpperBound - LowerBound; // e.g., (128+31) - 127 = 160 - 127 = 33
+            APInt NegRangeMinusOne = -Range - 1;   // e.g., -34? But expected -33
+
+            // Wait: the expected result is: icmp ult (x + -160), -33
+            // -160 = -LowerBound? LowerBound = 127 -> -127, not -160.
+
+            // Let's recompute with example:
+            // X != 127 and (X & -32) != 128
+            // (X & -32) != 128 means X not in [128, 159] (because 128 + 32 - 1 = 159)
+            // X != 127
+            // So overall: X not in [127, 159]
+            // That is: (X - 127) u> 32 (i.e., >= 33) -> (X - 127) u>= 33
+            // But we can write: (X - 127) u< 33 -> false => we want !(X - 127 u< 33)
+            // But that's not efficient.
+
+            // Instead, the expected code uses: (X - 160) u< -33
+            // -33 is 0xFFFFFFDF in i32, which is 4294967263, meaning the range [160, 160+4294967262] wraps.
+            // But that doesn't match.
+
+            // Let me reinterpret expected output:
+            // %1 = add i32 %x, -160
+            // %ret = icmp ult i32 %1, -33
+            // -33 is 0xFFFFFFDF = 4294967263
+            // So this is: (x - 160) <u 4294967263
+            // Which is true for x in [160, 0xFFFFFFDF + 159] = [160, 159 + 4294967263] -> wraps to [160, 159] (empty?) no.
+
+            // Actually: (x - 160) <u 4294967263 means all values except when (x-160) >= 4294967263
+            // i.e., x >= 160 + 4294967263 = 4294967423, and x < 160? No.
+
+            // Wait: 4294967263 = 2^32 - 33
+            // So (x - 160) <u (2^32 - 33)  =>  x - 160 in [0, 2^32-34]
+            // => x in [160, 159 + 2^32 - 33] = [160, 2^32 - 1] and [0, 126] (since 159 + 2^32 - 33 = 2^32 - 1, then next is 0 to 126 to complete?)
+            // No: it's just [160, 2^32-1] ∪ [0, 126]? But 160 + (2^32 - 34) = 2^32 - 34 + 160 = 2^32 + 126 -> wraps to 126.
+            // So yes: x in [160, 2^32-1] ∪ [0, 126] => x not in [127, 159] -> exactly what we want.
+
+            // So the condition (x-160) <u (2^32-33) is equivalent to x not in [127,159].
+
+            // Therefore: LowerBound = 127, UpperBound = 159, Size = 33.
+            // So we want: (x - (UpperBound+1)) <u (-Size)  => (x - 160) <u -33
+
+            APInt Size = UpperBound - LowerBound + 1; // 159 - 127 + 1 = 33
+            APInt StartAfter = UpperBound + 1; // 160
+
+            Value *Add = Builder.CreateAdd(X, ConstantInt::get(Ty, -StartAfter));
+            return Builder.CreateICmp(ICmpInst::ICMP_ULT, Add, ConstantInt::get(Ty, -Size));
+          }
+        }
+      }
+    }
+
     if (!(ICmp1->hasOneUse() && ICmp2->hasOneUse()) || CR1.isWrappedSet() ||
         CR2.isWrappedSet())
       return nullptr;
 
     // Check whether we have equal-size ranges that only differ by one bit.
     // In that case we can apply a mask to map one range onto the other.
+
     APInt LowerDiff = CR1.getLower() ^ CR2.getLower();
     APInt UpperDiff = (CR1.getUpper() - 1) ^ (CR2.getUpper() - 1);
     APInt CR1Size = CR1.getUpper() - CR1.getLower();
